• Key objectives include reducing logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to single digits, enhancing multimodal connectivity, and creating a data-driven decision support system.
• The NLP is expected to boost manufacturing, create employment opportunities, and improve the 'Ease of Doing Business' by creating a unified logistics ecosystem.
• It will facilitate faster last-mile delivery, reduce transaction costs, and enable better planning for perishable goods, benefiting farmers and consumers alike.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Logistics Policy (NLP).
Target for reducing logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
Key pillars/components of the NLP (e.g., ULIP, digital integration).
Related government initiatives like PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the National Logistics Policy in achieving India's economic growth targets and enhancing global competitiveness.
Discuss the challenges faced by India's logistics sector and how the new policy aims to address them.
Examine the potential impact of the NLP on various sectors such as manufacturing, agriculture, and e-commerce.
Critically evaluate the role of digital transformation and infrastructure development in the successful implementation of the NLP.
GS3.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected logistics networks, including roads, railways, and ports.
GS3.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest globally, valued at over $200 billion, and is expected to grow significantly with policy reforms.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a critical test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, marking a significant step towards cost-effective space access.
• The test involved a complex landing experiment simulating a re-entry scenario, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems.
• RLVs are crucial for reducing the cost of space missions by allowing the same launch vehicle components to be used multiple times, similar to aircraft.
• This achievement positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable space technologies, enhancing its capabilities in space exploration and commercial launches.
• Future plans include further tests on orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight, moving closer to operational deployment of the RLV system.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Purpose of Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLVs).
ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project.
Benefits of reusable technology in space exploration (cost reduction, sustainability).
Key challenges in RLV development (materials, guidance, thermal protection).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for global space competitiveness.
Analyze the potential economic and scientific benefits of reducing space launch costs through RLV technology.
Examine the technological challenges and innovations involved in designing and testing reusable launch vehicles.
Evaluate India's position in the global space race, highlighting its achievements and future aspirations in space technology.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype during a test flight.
GS3.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested its RLV-TD in a winged flight experiment in 2016, demonstrating hypersonic flight and autonomous landing.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious New Target for Green Hydrogen Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today announced an ambitious new national target for Green Hydrogen production, aiming to establish India as a global hub for this clean fuel.
• The revised target seeks to significantly ramp up India's annual green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, aligning with the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• This move is expected to attract substantial investments in renewable energy infrastructure, electrolyzer manufacturing, and associated supply chains.
• Green hydrogen, produced using renewable energy, is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and heavy transportation.
• The policy emphasizes incentives for domestic manufacturing, research & development, and pilot projects to foster a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Definition of Green Hydrogen vs. Grey/Blue Hydrogen.
Objectives of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Applications of Green Hydrogen (transport, industry, energy storage).
Role of renewable energy sources in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Discuss the technological, economic, and infrastructure challenges in scaling up Green Hydrogen production in India.
Evaluate the potential of India to become a global hub for Green Hydrogen production and export, outlining necessary policy support.
Examine the interlinkages between renewable energy expansion and the success of the Green Hydrogen Mission.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image showing a hydrogen molecule overlaid with a wind turbine and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: Unified Farmer Service Platform Launched Under Digital India Initiative
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today launched the 'Unified Farmer Service Platform' as part of the Digital India initiative, aiming to empower farmers with integrated digital services.
• The platform will provide a single window for farmers to access various government schemes, agricultural advisories, market information, and financial services.
• It integrates data from multiple sources, including land records, weather forecasts, crop patterns, and soil health cards, offering personalized recommendations.
• The initiative is expected to enhance transparency, reduce information asymmetry, and improve the efficiency of agricultural value chains.
• This digital push aims to modernize Indian agriculture, increase farmers' income, and ensure better access to resources and markets.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the Unified Farmer Service Platform.
Components and types of services offered (advisories, market data, schemes).
Role of e-governance in agriculture.
Other digital initiatives in agriculture (e.g., e-NAM, PM-KISAN).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital platforms and e-governance initiatives in transforming Indian agriculture and improving farmers' livelihoods.
Analyze the challenges in implementing large-scale digital solutions for farmers, considering issues like digital literacy and internet connectivity.
Evaluate how such unified platforms can address issues of market access, credit availability, and agricultural productivity.
Examine the role of data integration and personalized advisories in promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet to access digital agricultural services, with fields in the background.
GS3.trivia: India has the second-largest agricultural land in the world, cultivating a diverse range of crops across various agro-climatic zones.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Quarterly Inflation Report; Outlook Indicates Moderating Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its quarterly Inflation Report, providing an updated assessment of price stability and economic outlook.
• The report indicated a moderating trend in headline inflation, primarily driven by a softening of food prices and stable core inflation.
• The RBI maintained its stance on carefully monitoring global commodity prices and domestic demand pressures that could influence future inflation trajectory.
• Projections for the upcoming quarters suggest inflation is likely to remain within the tolerance band, though risks from volatile crude oil prices and erratic monsoons persist.
• The report's findings will be crucial for the Monetary Policy Committee's (MPC) future decisions on interest rates and liquidity management.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Functions of the RBI (monetary policy, inflation targeting).
Role and composition of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Difference between CPI and WPI.
Factors influencing inflation (demand-pull, cost-push, global factors).
RBI's inflation target band.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current inflation trends in India and the factors contributing to their moderation or persistence.
Discuss the tools and strategies employed by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee to manage inflation and maintain price stability.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Evaluate the impact of global economic developments and domestic agricultural output on India's inflation trajectory.
GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing a downward trend in inflation, with the RBI logo in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: New Study Highlights Increasing Coastal Erosion Threats to Indian Shorelines
GS3.explainer:
• A new scientific study published today warns of increasing coastal erosion along significant stretches of India's shoreline, posing a severe threat to ecosystems and coastal communities.
• The study, using satellite imagery and ground surveys, identified several highly vulnerable regions, particularly in states like West Bengal, Odisha, Kerala, and Gujarat.
• Factors contributing to accelerated erosion include rising sea levels, frequent extreme weather events, anthropogenic activities like sand mining, and inadequate coastal zone management.
• The loss of mangroves, coral reefs, and other natural coastal defenses further exacerbates the problem, impacting biodiversity and livelihoods.
• Experts call for urgent implementation of integrated coastal zone management plans, nature-based solutions, and relocation strategies for at-risk populations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Major causes of coastal erosion in India.
Vulnerable Indian states/regions to coastal erosion.
Impacts of coastal erosion (environmental, economic, social).
Role of mangroves and coral reefs in coastal protection.
Concept of Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM).
mainsPoints: Analyze the drivers and impacts of coastal erosion on India's diverse coastline, considering both natural and anthropogenic factors.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective coastal zone management strategies and suggest integrated approaches to mitigate erosion.
Evaluate the role of nature-based solutions (e.g., mangrove restoration) in enhancing coastal resilience against climate change impacts.
Examine the socio-economic implications of coastal erosion for fishing communities and coastal infrastructure.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a coastline showing significant land loss due to erosion, with waves crashing.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of about 7,516.6 km, including the mainland and island territories, making it highly susceptible to coastal processes.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Draft Framework for Ethical AI Development and Deployment
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today released a draft framework for the ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The framework aims to ensure that AI systems are fair, transparent, accountable, secure, and respectful of privacy and human rights.
• It proposes guidelines for data governance, algorithmic bias detection, human oversight, and mechanisms for redressal in case of AI-related harm.
• Public consultation has been invited on the draft, seeking inputs from industry experts, academia, civil society, and other stakeholders.
• This move signals India's commitment to fostering responsible AI innovation while mitigating potential risks associated with advanced AI technologies.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Key principles of ethical AI (fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy).
Challenges of AI (bias, privacy, job displacement).
Government bodies involved in AI policy (MeitY, NITI Aayog).
Related legislation like the Digital Personal Data Protection Act.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges and societal implications arising from the rapid development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in India.
Analyze the key features of the proposed ethical AI framework and its potential to ensure responsible AI innovation.
Examine the balance between fostering AI innovation and mitigating risks such as algorithmic bias, job displacement, and privacy concerns.
Evaluate the role of government, industry, and civil society in building a trustworthy and human-centric AI ecosystem in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a human hand shaking a robotic hand, with lines representing data flow, symbolizing AI ethics.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing AI markets globally, with significant applications in healthcare, finance, and e-governance.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
